initial_program:
% ---------- Grid dimensions ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% ---------- Colour facts ----------
color(V) :- input(R, C, V).

% ---------- Max colour ----------
1 { MaxC = V } 1 :- color(V).          % guess a colour that appears
:- color(V), V > MaxC.                  % nothing larger than MaxC may appear

% ---------- Translation offsets (one per colour) ----------
% dr_4  dr_9 and dc_4  dc_9 are integer variables.
1 { dr_0 = D0 } 1 { dr_1 = D1 } 1 { dr_2 = D2 } 1 { dr_3 = D3 }
1 { dr_4 = D4 } 1 { dr_5 = D5 } 1 { dr_6 = D6 } 1 { dr_7 = D7 }
1 { dr_8 = D8 } 1 { dr_9 = D9 }.
1 { dc_0 = D0c } 1 { dc_1 = D1c } 1 { dc_2 = D2c } 1 { dc_3 = D3c }
1 { dc_4 = D4c } 1 { dc_5 = D5c } 1 { dc_6 = D6c } 1 { dc_7 = D7c }
1 { dc_8 = D8c } 1 { dc_9 = D9c }.

% Range for the offsets
dr_0 >= -maxDim.  dr_0 <= maxDim.
dr_1 >= -maxDim.  dr_1 <= maxDim.
dr_2 >= -maxDim.  dr_2 <= maxDim.
dr_3 >= -maxDim.  dr_3 <= maxDim.
dr_4 >= -maxDim.  dr_4 <= maxDim.
dr_5 >= -maxDim.  dr_5 <= maxDim.
dr_6 >= -maxDim.  dr_6 <= maxDim.
dr_7 >= -maxDim.  dr_7 <= maxDim.
dr_8 >= -maxDim.  dr_8 <= maxDim.
dr_9 >= -maxDim.  dr_9 <= maxDim.

dc_0 >= -maxDim.  dc_0 <= maxDim.
dc_1 >= -maxDim.  dc_1 <= maxDim.
dc_2 >= -maxDim.  dc_2 <= maxDim.
dc_3 >= -maxDim.  dc_3 <= maxDim.
dc_4 >= -maxDim.  dc_4 <= maxDim.
dc_5 >= -maxDim.  dc_5 <= maxDim.
dc_6 >= -maxDim.  dc_6 <= maxDim.
dc_7 >= -maxDim.  dc_7 <= maxDim.
dc_8 >= -maxDim.  dc_8 <= maxDim.
dc_9 >= -maxDim.  dc_9 <= maxDim.

% ---------- Output layout ----------
% New position of an input cell is obtained by adding the
% colourspecific offset (dr_C , dc_C) to its original coordinates.
output(R2, C2, C) :- input(R, C, C), R2 = R + dr_C, C2 = C + dc_C.

% Output dimensions (derived from the output itself)
N_out = #max{ R : output(R, _, _) } + 1.
M_out = #max{ C : output(_, C, _) } + 1.

% ---------- Layer constraints ----------
% Every cell that belongs to colour C must lie in the layer
% that corresponds to the target distance (MaxCC).
:-
    forall(R, C, C0) input(R, C, C0) ->
        let R2 = R + dr_C,  let C2 = C + dc_C,
        (R2 >= MaxC - C0) &
        (R2 <= N_out - 1 - (MaxC - C0)) &
        (C2 >= MaxC - C0) &
        (C2 <= M_out - 1 - (MaxC - C0)).

% Anchor condition: for each colour C that really occurs
% at least one of its cells must touch the layer exactly
% (i.e. its row **or** its column equals the target distance).
1 { anchor(C) } 1 :- color(C), C > 0.      % we really have this colour
:- anchor(C),
   not ( exists(R,C0) input(R, C0, C) with R + dr_C = MaxC - C ) &
   not ( exists(R,C0) input(R, C0, C) with C + dc_C = MaxC - C ).

% ---------- Exactly one colour per cell ----------
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
output_cell(R, C) :- output(R, C, _).
